19	# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
20	# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
21	#
22	# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
23	# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
24	# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced
25	
26	set -euo pipefail
27	
28	# Multiple inclusion detection
29	test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
30	ZRBOB_SOURCED=1
31	
32	
33	######################################################################
34	# Compose-env Validation
35	
36	# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
37	# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
38	# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
39	# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
40	zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
41	  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
42	  local z_compose_fields="
43	    RBRN_MONIKER
44	    RBRN_RUNTIME
45	    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
46	    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
47	    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
48	    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
49	    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
50	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
51	    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
52	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
53	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
54	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
55	    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
56	    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
57	    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
58	    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
59	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
60	    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
61	  "
62	
63	  local z_field=""
64	  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
65	    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
66	    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
67	    case "${z_value}" in
68	      *\"*|*\'*)
69	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
70	        ;;
71	    esac
72	    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
73	    case "${z_value}" in
74	      *'${'*)
75	        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
76	        ;;
77	    esac
78	  done
79	}
80	
81	######################################################################
82	# Kindle and Sentinel
83	
84	zrbob_kindle() {
85	  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"
86	
87	  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
88	  zrbrn_sentinel
89	
90	  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
91	  zrbob_validate_compose_env
92	
93	  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
94	  zrbrr_sentinel
95	
96	  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
97	  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
98	    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
99	    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
100	    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
101	  esac
102	
103	  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
104	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry"
105	  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle"
106	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle"
107	
108	  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
109	  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${RBRN_MONIKER}_enclave"
110	
111	  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
112	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
113	  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"
114	
115	  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
116	  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time
117	
118	  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
119	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
120	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
121	  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"
122	
123	
124	  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
125	  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
126	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
127	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
128	
129	  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
130	  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
131	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
132	
133	  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
134	  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
135	  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
136	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
137	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
138	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
139	  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
140	  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
141	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
142	  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
143	  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
144	  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
145	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
146	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
147	  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
148	  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
149	  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE
150	
151	  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
152	  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
153	  local z_bottle_user=""
154	  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
155	    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
156	  fi
157	  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
158	  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
159	
160	  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"
161	
162	  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
163	}
164	
165	zrbob_sentinel() {
166	  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
167	}
168	
169	######################################################################
170	# Compose Command Helper
171	
172	# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
173	# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
174	zrbob_compose() {
175	  zrbob_sentinel
176	
177	  local z_args=()
178	  z_args+=("compose")
179	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
180	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
181	  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
182	  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")
183	
184	  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
185	  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
186	    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
187	  fi
188	
189	  z_args+=("-p" "${RBRN_MONIKER}")
190	  z_args+=("$@")
191	
192	  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
193	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
194	}
195	
196	######################################################################
197	# Auto-Summon Helper
198	
199	# Verify vouch exists and pull image if missing locally
200	# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
201	zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
202	  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
203	  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
204	  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"
205	
206	  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
207	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
208	  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"
209	
210	  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
211	  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
212	
213	  local z_token
214	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"
215	
216	  # Pull the image
217	  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"
218	
219	  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
220	    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"
221	
222	  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"
223	
224	  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
225	}
226	
227	######################################################################
228	# Subnet Conflict Detection
229	
230	# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
231	# This catches stale networks from prior compose projects that died without
232	# compose-down (reboot, Docker Desktop restart, crash). Our own network
233	# (from the compose-down above) is excluded.
234	zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
235	  zrbob_sentinel
236	
237	  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
238	  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
239	  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"
240	
241	  # List all Docker network names
242	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
243	    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"
244	
245	  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
246	  local z_names=()
247	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
248	    z_names+=("${z_line}")
249	  done < "${z_networks_file}"
250	
251	  # Inspect each network's subnet
252	  local z_name=""
253	  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
254	    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
255	    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue
256	
257	    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
258	      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
259	      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue
260	
261	    local z_subnet=""
262	    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")
263	
264	    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
265	      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
266	    fi
267	  done
268	}
269	
270	######################################################################
271	# Public API
272	
273	# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
274	# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
275	rbob_charge() {
276	  zrbob_sentinel
277	
278	  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
279	
280	  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
281	  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
282	    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
283	  fi
284	
285	  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
286	  rbrn_preflight
287	
288	  # Preflight: verify vouch artifacts exist locally — no network calls, fatal if missing
289	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
290	    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
291	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
292	    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
293	  fi
294	
295	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
296	    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
297	    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
298	    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
299	  fi
300	
301	  # Preflight: verify container images exist locally, auto-summon if missing
302	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
303	    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
304	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
305	  fi
306	
307	  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
308	    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
309	    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
310	  fi
311	
312	  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
313	  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
314	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true
315	
316	  # Defensive check: another compose project may hold a network on our subnet
317	  # (e.g., nsproto containers died without compose-down, blocking tadmor's subnet)
318	  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict
319	
320	  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
321	  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
322	  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
323	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait
324	
325	  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
326	}
327	
328	# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
329	# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
330	rbob_charged_predicate() {
331	  zrbob_sentinel
332	  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${RBRN_MONIKER}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
333	}
334	
335	# Stop the crucible via compose
336	rbob_quench() {
337	  zrbob_sentinel
338	
339	  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
340	
341	  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans
342	
343	  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
344	}
345	
346	# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
347	rbob_hail() {
348	  zrbob_sentinel
349	  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
350	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
351	}
352	
353	# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
354	rbob_ssh() {
355	  zrbob_sentinel
356	  local z_ssh_user="${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER:-root}"
357	  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} as ${z_ssh_user}"
358	  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
359	    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
360	    "${z_ssh_user}@localhost"
361	}
362	
363	# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
364	rbob_rack() {
365	  zrbob_sentinel
366	  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
367	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
368	}
369	
370	# Writ sentry — non-interactive command execution in sentry
371	rbob_writ() {
372	  zrbob_sentinel
373	  buc_step "Writ to sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
374	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" "$@"
375	}
376	
377	# Fiat pentacle — non-interactive command execution in pentacle
378	rbob_fiat() {
379	  zrbob_sentinel
380	  buc_step "Fiat to pentacle: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
381	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" "$@"
382	}
383	
384	# Bark bottle — non-interactive command execution in bottle
385	rbob_bark() {
386	  zrbob_sentinel
387	  buc_step "Bark to bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
388	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" "$@"
389	}
390	
391	# Launch Claude Code client inside bottle (ifrit engagement)
392	rbob_ifrit_client() {
393	  zrbob_sentinel
394	  buc_step "Launching Claude Code client in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
395	  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" claude
396	}
397	
398	# Run ifrit sortie adjutant inside bottle (security test dispatch)
399	rbob_ifrit_sortie() {
400	  zrbob_sentinel
401	  buc_step "Running ifrit sortie adjutant in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
402	  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" \
403	    python3 rbtia_adjutant.py "$@"
404	}
405	
406	######################################################################
407	# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
408	# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark
409	
410	zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
411	  local -r z_file="$1"
412	  local -r z_var_name="$2"
413	  local -r z_new_value="$3"
414	
415	  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
416	  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
417	  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"
418	
419	  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
420	  local z_lines=()
421	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
422	    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
423	  done < "${z_file}"
424	
425	  # Rewrite with substitution
426	  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
427	  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"
428	
429	  local z_found=0
430	  local z_i="" z_current=""
431	  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
432	    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
433	    case "${z_current}" in
434	      "${z_var_name}"=*)
435	        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
436	        z_found=1
437	        ;;
438	    esac
439	    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
440	  done
441	
442	  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"
443	
444	  # Atomic replace
445	  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"
446	
447	  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
448	}
449	
450	######################################################################
451	# Kludge Bottle — param1 dispatch target delegating to rbob_kludge
452	
453	rbob_kludge_bottle() {
454	  zrbob_sentinel
455	  rbob_kludge
456	}
457	
458	######################################################################
459	# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate
460	
461	rbob_kludge() {
462	  zrbob_sentinel
463	  zrbfc_sentinel
464	
465	  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
466	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
467	
468	  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
469	
470	  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
471	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
472	  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"
473	
474	  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
475	  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"
476	
477	  # Read hallmark from fact file
478	  local z_hallmark=""
479	  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
480	    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
481	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"
482	
483	  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
484	  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"
485	
486	  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
487	}
488	
489	######################################################################
490	# Ordain — cloud-build bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate
491	
492	rbob_ordain() {
493	  zrbob_sentinel
494	  zrbfd_sentinel
495	
496	  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
497	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
498	
499	  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"
500	
501	  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
502	  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
503	  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"
504	
505	  # Delegate to foundry ordain (mode-dispatched: conjure, bind, or graft)
506	  rbfd_ordain "${z_vessel_dir}"
507	
508	  # Read hallmark from fact file
509	  local z_hallmark=""
510	  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
511	    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
512	  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"
513	
514	  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
515	  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"
516	
517	  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
518	}

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# RBOB - Recipe Bottle Orchestration Bottle
# Container lifecycle management via Docker Compose
#
# Requires: buc_command.sh sourced
# Requires: rbrn_regime.sh sourced
# Requires: rbrr_regime.sh sourced

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBOB_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBOB_SOURCED=1


######################################################################
# Compose-env Validation

# Validate that compose-consumed RBRN fields contain no quotes or ${VAR} references.
# Compose --env-file does not strip quotes (they become part of the value) and
# interpolates ${VAR} references (causing unintended expansion). Fields not consumed
# by compose (e.g., RBRN_DESCRIPTION) are exempt.
zrbob_validate_compose_env() {
  # Explicit list of compose-consumed field names (the nameplate↔compose contract)
  local z_compose_fields="
    RBRN_MONIKER
    RBRN_RUNTIME
    RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL
    RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL
    RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK
    RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK
    RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
    RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
    RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
    RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
    RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
    RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  "

  local z_field=""
  for z_field in ${z_compose_fields}; do
    local z_value="${!z_field:-}"
    # Check for quotes (compose does not strip them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *\"*|*\'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains quotes — compose --env-file does not strip quotes, they become part of the value"
        ;;
    esac
    # Check for ${VAR} references (compose interpolates them)
    case "${z_value}" in
      *'${'*)
        buc_die "Compose compatibility: ${z_field} contains \${VAR} reference — compose --env-file interpolates these, causing unintended expansion"
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

######################################################################
# Kindle and Sentinel

zrbob_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbob already kindled"

  # Verify RBRN regime is kindled (provides nameplate config)
  zrbrn_sentinel

  # Validate compose-consumed fields before proceeding
  zrbob_validate_compose_env

  # Verify RBRR regime is kindled (provides repo config like DNS_SERVER)
  zrbrr_sentinel

  # Runtime command (docker or podman)
  case "${RBRN_RUNTIME}" in
    docker) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="docker" ;;
    podman) readonly ZRBOB_RUNTIME="podman" ;;
    *) buc_die "Unknown RBRN_RUNTIME: ${RBRN_RUNTIME}" ;;
  esac

  # Container names (for connect and info commands)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY="${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry"
  readonly ZRBOB_PENTACLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE="${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle"

  # Network name (compose names as {project}_{network}; used by observe and info)
  readonly ZRBOB_NETWORK="${RBRN_MONIKER}_enclave"

  # Compose file paths (relative to project root, where compose runs)
  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbob_compose.yml"
  test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}" || buc_die "Base compose file not found: ${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}"

  readonly ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/compose.yml"
  # Fragment is optional — existence checked at compose invocation time

  # Env file paths (for compose --env-file: YAML interpolation + container env forwarding)
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbrr.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE="${RBBC_dot_dir}/rbje_compose_probe.env"
  readonly ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN="${RBBC_dot_dir}/${RBRN_MONIKER}/${RBCC_rbrn_file}"


  # GAR image references (computed once, used by preflight and auto-summon)
  local z_gar_base="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}/${RBGD_GAR_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}"
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_IMAGE}"

  # GAR vouch references (local presence verified on every start)
  readonly ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH="${z_gar_base}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}${RBGC_ARK_SUFFIX_VOUCH}"

  # Export RBRN/RBRR vars for compose environment: bare name forwarding.
  # Compose --env-file populates the compose environment for both YAML interpolation
  # and bare name forwarding; explicit exports provide defense-in-depth.
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
  export RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
  export RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
  export RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
  export RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
  export RBRR_DNS_SERVER
  export RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE

  # Load bottle vessel user for compose and SSH (optional — empty means image default)
  local z_bottle_rbrv="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}/rbrv.env"
  local z_bottle_user=""
  if test -f "${z_bottle_rbrv}"; then
    z_bottle_user=$(grep '^RBRV_USER=' "${z_bottle_rbrv}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2) || true
  fi
  readonly ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER="${z_bottle_user}"
  export RBRV_USER="${z_bottle_user}"

  readonly ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_drive_"

  readonly ZRBOB_KINDLED=1
}

zrbob_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBOB_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbob not kindled - call zrbob_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Compose Command Helper

# Build and execute a compose command with standard args
# Usage: zrbob_compose <compose_subcommand> [args...]
zrbob_compose() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_args=()
  z_args+=("compose")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRR}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBJE}")
  z_args+=("--env-file" "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}")
  z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_BASE}")

  # Include nameplate fragment if it exists
  if test -f "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}"; then
    z_args+=("-f" "${ZRBOB_COMPOSE_FRAGMENT}")
  fi

  z_args+=("-p" "${RBRN_MONIKER}")
  z_args+=("$@")

  buc_log_args "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} ${z_args[*]}"
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" "${z_args[@]}"
}

######################################################################
# Auto-Summon Helper

# Verify vouch exists and pull image if missing locally
# Usage: zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon <vessel> <hallmark> <image_ref>
zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon() {
  local z_vessel="${1:-}"
  local z_hallmark="${2:-}"
  local z_image_ref="${3:-}"

  test -n "${z_vessel}"       || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: vessel required"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: hallmark required"
  test -n "${z_image_ref}"    || buc_die "zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon: image_ref required"

  local z_registry_host="${RBGD_GAR_LOCATION}${RBGC_GAR_HOST_SUFFIX}"
  test -f "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}" || buc_die "Retriever credential not found: ${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"

  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to get OAuth token for auto-summon"

  # Pull the image
  buc_step "Auto-summoning ${z_image_ref}"

  echo "${z_token}" | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_registry_host}" \
    || buc_die "Container registry authentication failed during auto-summon"

  docker pull "${z_image_ref}" || buc_die "Failed to pull image: ${z_image_ref}"

  buc_info "Auto-summoned: ${z_image_ref}"
}

######################################################################
# Subnet Conflict Detection

# Die if another Docker network already occupies this nameplate's subnet.
# This catches stale networks from prior compose projects that died without
# compose-down (reboot, Docker Desktop restart, crash). Our own network
# (from the compose-down above) is excluded.
zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  local z_target_subnet="${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}"
  local z_networks_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_networks.txt"
  local z_subnet_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/zrbob_detect_subnet.txt"

  # List all Docker network names
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network ls --format '{{.Name}}' > "${z_networks_file}" \
    || buc_die "Failed to list Docker networks"

  # Load network names (load-then-iterate per BCG)
  local z_names=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_names+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_networks_file}"

  # Inspect each network's subnet
  local z_name=""
  for z_name in "${z_names[@]}"; do
    # Skip our own compose network — compose down already handled it
    test "${z_name}" != "${ZRBOB_NETWORK}" || continue

    ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} network inspect "${z_name}" \
      --format '{{range .IPAM.Config}}{{.Subnet}}{{end}}' \
      > "${z_subnet_file}" 2>/dev/null || continue

    local z_subnet=""
    z_subnet=$(<"${z_subnet_file}")

    if test "${z_subnet}" = "${z_target_subnet}"; then
      buc_die "Subnet ${z_target_subnet} already claimed by stale network '${z_name}' — clean up manually: docker rm \$(docker ps -aq --filter network=${z_name}) 2>/dev/null; docker network rm ${z_name}"
    fi
  done
}

######################################################################
# Public API

# Start the crucible (sentry + pentacle + bottle) via compose
# Requires: RBOB kindled (which requires RBRN and RBRR)
rbob_charge() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Starting crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  # Gate: nameplate must have no uncommitted changes
  if ! git diff --quiet -- "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" 2>/dev/null; then
    buc_die "Nameplate has uncommitted changes: ${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN} — commit before charging"
  fi

  # Cross-nameplate validation (silent on success, dies on conflict)
  rbrn_preflight

  # Preflight: verify vouch artifacts exist locally — no network calls, fatal if missing
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_VOUCH}"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}"
    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle vouch artifact missing locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_VOUCH}"
    buc_tabtarget "${RBZ_SUMMON_HALLMARK}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}"
    buc_die "Run summon to pull vouch artifact before starting"
  fi

  # Preflight: verify container images exist locally, auto-summon if missing
  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Sentry image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_SENTRY_IMAGE}"
  fi

  if ! ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} image inspect "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    buc_warn "Bottle image not found locally: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
    zrbob_vouch_gate_and_summon "${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}" "${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_IMAGE}"
  fi

  # Tear down any prior state (tolerates missing project)
  buc_step "Cleaning up any prior state"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans 2>/dev/null || true

  # Defensive check: another compose project may hold a network on our subnet
  # (e.g., nsproto containers died without compose-down, blocking tadmor's subnet)
  zrbob_detect_subnet_conflict

  # Start services via compose (--profile sessile includes bottle)
  # --wait blocks until all health checks pass (sentry → pentacle → bottle chain)
  buc_step "Starting services via compose"
  zrbob_compose --profile sessile up -d --wait

  buc_step "Crucible started: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Check whether the crucible is charged (compose project has running containers).
# BCG predicate: returns 0 if charged, 1 if not. Never dies, no output.
rbob_charged_predicate() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  "${ZRBOB_RUNTIME}" compose -p "${RBRN_MONIKER}" ps -q --status running 2>/dev/null | grep -q .
}

# Stop the crucible via compose
rbob_quench() {
  zrbob_sentinel

  buc_step "Stopping crucible: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"

  zrbob_compose --profile sessile down --remove-orphans

  buc_step "Crucible stopped: ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# Hail sentry — call out to the guard (interactive shell)
rbob_hail() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Hailing sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" /bin/bash
}

# SSH into bottle — proper terminal session via sentry DNAT
rbob_ssh() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  local z_ssh_user="${ZRBOB_BOTTLE_USER:-root}"
  buc_step "SSH to bottle via port ${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION} as ${z_ssh_user}"
  exec ssh -t -p "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}" \
    -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null \
    "${z_ssh_user}@localhost"
}

# Rack bottle — compel the demon to reveal state (interactive shell)
rbob_rack() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Racking bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" /bin/bash
}

# Writ sentry — non-interactive command execution in sentry
rbob_writ() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Writ to sentry: ${ZRBOB_SENTRY}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_SENTRY}" "$@"
}

# Fiat pentacle — non-interactive command execution in pentacle
rbob_fiat() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Fiat to pentacle: ${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_PENTACLE}" "$@"
}

# Bark bottle — non-interactive command execution in bottle
rbob_bark() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Bark to bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" "$@"
}

# Launch Claude Code client inside bottle (ifrit engagement)
rbob_ifrit_client() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Launching Claude Code client in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  exec ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -it -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" claude
}

# Run ifrit sortie adjutant inside bottle (security test dispatch)
rbob_ifrit_sortie() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  buc_step "Running ifrit sortie adjutant in bottle: ${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}"
  ${ZRBOB_RUNTIME} exec -w "${RBRR_BOTTLE_WORKSPACE}" "${ZRBOB_BOTTLE}" \
    python3 rbtia_adjutant.py "$@"
}

######################################################################
# Drive Hallmark — rewrite a single RBRN_*_HALLMARK line in nameplate env
# Args: nameplate_file variable_name new_hallmark

zrbob_drive_hallmark() {
  local -r z_file="$1"
  local -r z_var_name="$2"
  local -r z_new_value="$3"

  test -f "${z_file}" || buc_die "Nameplate file not found: ${z_file}"
  test -n "${z_var_name}" || buc_die "Variable name required"
  test -n "${z_new_value}" || buc_die "New hallmark value required"

  # Load file into array (BCG load-then-iterate)
  local z_lines=()
  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
    z_lines+=("${z_line}")
  done < "${z_file}"

  # Rewrite with substitution
  local -r z_temp="${ZRBOB_DRIVE_PREFIX}${z_var_name}"
  : > "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to create temp file: ${z_temp}"

  local z_found=0
  local z_i="" z_current=""
  for z_i in "${!z_lines[@]}"; do
    z_current="${z_lines[$z_i]}"
    case "${z_current}" in
      "${z_var_name}"=*)
        z_current="${z_var_name}=${z_new_value}"
        z_found=1
        ;;
    esac
    printf '%s\n' "${z_current}" >> "${z_temp}" || buc_die "Failed to write line"
  done

  test "${z_found}" = "1" || buc_die "Variable ${z_var_name} not found in ${z_file}"

  # Atomic replace
  mv "${z_temp}" "${z_file}" || buc_die "Failed to replace nameplate file: ${z_file}"

  buc_info "Drove ${z_var_name}=${z_new_value} → ${z_file}"
}

######################################################################
# Kludge Bottle — param1 dispatch target delegating to rbob_kludge

rbob_kludge_bottle() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  rbob_kludge
}

######################################################################
# Kludge — build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_kludge() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfc_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Build bottle vessel locally and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Kludge: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate build to foundry kludge
  rbfd_kludge "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from kludge output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from kludge output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Kludge installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

######################################################################
# Ordain — cloud-build bottle vessel and drive hallmark into nameplate

rbob_ordain() {
  zrbob_sentinel
  zrbfd_sentinel

  buc_doc_brief "Ordain bottle vessel via cloud build and drive hallmark into nameplate"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_step "Ordain: ${RBRN_MONIKER} (${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL})"

  # Resolve vessel from nameplate config
  local -r z_vessel_dir="${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}"
  test -d "${z_vessel_dir}" || buc_die "Bottle vessel directory not found: ${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Delegate to foundry ordain (mode-dispatched: conjure, bind, or graft)
  rbfd_ordain "${z_vessel_dir}"

  # Read hallmark from fact file
  local z_hallmark=""
  z_hallmark=$(<"${BURD_OUTPUT_DIR}/${RBF_FACT_HALLMARK}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to read hallmark from ordain output"
  test -n "${z_hallmark}" || buc_die "Empty hallmark from ordain output"

  # Drive hallmark into nameplate
  zrbob_drive_hallmark "${ZRBOB_ENV_RBRN}" "RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK" "${z_hallmark}"

  buc_success "Ordain installed: ${z_hallmark} → ${RBRN_MONIKER}"
}

# eof

1	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
2	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
3	#
4	# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
5	#
6	# Invoked via:
7	#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
8	#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
9	#
10	# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
11	#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
12	#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
13	#
14	# Network topology:
15	#   sentry: default (internet via bridge) + enclave (eth0=default priority:1000, eth1=enclave priority:500)
16	#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
17	#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
18	#
19	# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)
20

file.content:
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
#
# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
#
# Invoked via:
#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
#
# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
#
# Network topology:
#   sentry: default (internet via bridge) + enclave (eth0=default priority:1000, eth1=enclave priority:500)
#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
#
# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)

services:
  sentry:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
    privileged: true
    networks:
      default:
        priority: 1000
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
        priority: 500
    ports:
      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
      - RBJE_PROBE
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  pentacle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
    privileged: true
    networks:
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
    depends_on:
      sentry:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  bottle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
    security_opt:
      - label:disable
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
    depends_on:
      pentacle:
        condition: service_healthy
    profiles:
      - sessile

networks:
  enclave:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure claude user home is ready
CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)

# Seed persistent workspace with sample project on first boot.
# The volume is empty on initial charge; subsequent charges reuse it.
WORKSPACE="${CLAUDE_HOME}/workspace"
if [ -d /opt/ccyolo-sample ] && [ -d "${WORKSPACE}" ] && [ -z "$(ls -A "${WORKSPACE}" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  cp -a /opt/ccyolo-sample/. "${WORKSPACE}/"
  chmod +x "${WORKSPACE}"/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true
fi

chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

# Start SSH daemon in foreground (container lifecycle tied to sshd)
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D

1	# ccyolo — Claude Code sandbox bottle for Recipe Bottle crucible
2	#
3	# SSH-accessible container running Claude Code. The sentry DNAT
4	# forwards ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION to this container's port 22.
5	# User SSHs in as "claude" (empty password, PAM-permissive),
6	# then runs `claude` to authenticate via OAuth copy/paste flow.
7	#
8	# Build context: rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/ (co-located with Dockerfile)
9	
10	ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
11	FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}
12	
13	# Avoid prompts from apt
14	ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive
15	
16	# Install SSH server and Claude Code operational dependencies
17	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
18	    openssh-server    `# SSH daemon for workstation entry via sentry DNAT`  \
19	    ca-certificates   `# TLS root certs — required for HTTPS to Anthropic API` \
20	    curl              `# HTTP client (Claude Code operational dependency)`  \
21	    git               `# Version control (Claude Code operational dependency)` \
22	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
23	
24	# Install Claude Code globally via npm (node and npm provided by base image)
25	# Pin version — v2.1.105+ has a bracketed paste regression that breaks
26	# OAuth code entry through SSH/docker-exec terminals.
27	RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.89
28	
29	# Create unprivileged claude user with empty password
30	RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash claude && \
31	    passwd -d claude
32	
33	# Generate SSH host keys
34	RUN ssh-keygen -A
35	
36	# Configure SSH daemon
37	RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd                                       && \
38	    echo "Port 22"                    >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
39	    echo "PermitRootLogin no"         >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
40	    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords yes"   >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
41	    echo "PasswordAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config
42	
43	# Configure PAM for permissive authentication (no password prompt)
44	RUN sed -i 's/@include common-auth/#@include common-auth/' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
45	    echo "auth sufficient pam_permit.so" >> /etc/pam.d/sshd
46	
47	# Ensure PATH includes npm global bin for SSH sessions
48	RUN echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc
49	
50	# Preconfigure Claude Code for full autonomy (yolo mode).
51	# Inside a network-contained crucible, bypassing permission prompts is
52	# the correct posture — the sentry enforces the real security boundary.
53	RUN mkdir -p /home/claude/.claude && \
54	    echo '{"permissions":{"defaultMode":"bypassPermissions"}}' \
55	      > /home/claude/.claude/settings.json && \
56	    chown -R claude:claude /home/claude/.claude
57	
58	# Stage sample project as a template — the entrypoint seeds the
59	# persistent workspace volume on first boot.
60	COPY sample-project/ /opt/ccyolo-sample/
61	
62	COPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
63	RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
64	
65	EXPOSE 22
66	
67	USER root
68	ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]
69

file.content:
# ccyolo — Claude Code sandbox bottle for Recipe Bottle crucible
#
# SSH-accessible container running Claude Code. The sentry DNAT
# forwards ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION to this container's port 22.
# User SSHs in as "claude" (empty password, PAM-permissive),
# then runs `claude` to authenticate via OAuth copy/paste flow.
#
# Build context: rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/ (co-located with Dockerfile)

ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

# Avoid prompts from apt
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install SSH server and Claude Code operational dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    openssh-server    `# SSH daemon for workstation entry via sentry DNAT`  \
    ca-certificates   `# TLS root certs — required for HTTPS to Anthropic API` \
    curl              `# HTTP client (Claude Code operational dependency)`  \
    git               `# Version control (Claude Code operational dependency)` \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Claude Code globally via npm (node and npm provided by base image)
# Pin version — v2.1.105+ has a bracketed paste regression that breaks
# OAuth code entry through SSH/docker-exec terminals.
RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.89

# Create unprivileged claude user with empty password
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash claude && \
    passwd -d claude

# Generate SSH host keys
RUN ssh-keygen -A

# Configure SSH daemon
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd                                       && \
    echo "Port 22"                    >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitRootLogin no"         >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords yes"   >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PasswordAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Configure PAM for permissive authentication (no password prompt)
RUN sed -i 's/@include common-auth/#@include common-auth/' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
    echo "auth sufficient pam_permit.so" >> /etc/pam.d/sshd

# Ensure PATH includes npm global bin for SSH sessions
RUN echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# Preconfigure Claude Code for full autonomy (yolo mode).
# Inside a network-contained crucible, bypassing permission prompts is
# the correct posture — the sentry enforces the real security boundary.
RUN mkdir -p /home/claude/.claude && \
    echo '{"permissions":{"defaultMode":"bypassPermissions"}}' \
      > /home/claude/.claude/settings.json && \
    chown -R claude:claude /home/claude/.claude

# Stage sample project as a template — the entrypoint seeds the
# persistent workspace volume on first boot.
COPY sample-project/ /opt/ccyolo-sample/

COPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 22

USER root
ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
# count_words.sh — Count unique words in a text file
# Usage: ./count_words.sh <filename>

if [ -z "$1" ]; then
    echo "Usage: $0 <filename>"
    exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$1" ]; then
    echo "Error: File '$1' not found"
    exit 1
fi

echo "=== Word Frequency Analysis ==="
echo "File: $1"
echo

# Count unique words and sort by frequency (descending)
cat "$1" \
  | tr -s '[:space:]' '\n' \
  | sort \
  | uniq -c \
  | sort -rn \
  | head -20

echo
total_unique=$(cat "$1" | tr -s '[:space:]' '\n' | sort | uniq | wc -l)
total_words=$(cat "$1" | wc -w)

echo "Total unique words: ${total_unique}"
echo "Total words:        ${total_words}"

content.content:
1	The Recipe Bottle project builds sandboxed container environments where
2	AI agents can operate with full autonomy. The key insight is that network
3	containment makes autonomy safe — if the container can only reach the AI
4	provider, the agent cannot exfiltrate data or cause harm to external
5	systems.
6	
7	A Crucible is the running instance of this containment. The Sentry
8	enforces network rules, the Pentacle establishes the namespace, and the
9	Bottle holds the workload. When you charge a Crucible, all three
10	containers start in sequence — the Sentry must confirm its iptables
11	rules before the Bottle can begin.
12	
13	The project uses a vocabulary-first approach: every concept has a
14	specific name, and the names carry meaning. A Vessel is a container
15	specification. A Hallmark is a specific build. A Nameplate ties the
16	pieces together. The vocabulary is dense but precise — once you learn
17	the names, the system becomes navigable.
18	
19	This is the ccyolo bottle — the simplest path into the system. You are
20	inside a container that can only reach Anthropic. Everything else is
21	blocked. The sentry is watching. The containment is real.
22

file.content:
The Recipe Bottle project builds sandboxed container environments where
AI agents can operate with full autonomy. The key insight is that network
containment makes autonomy safe — if the container can only reach the AI
provider, the agent cannot exfiltrate data or cause harm to external
systems.

A Crucible is the running instance of this containment. The Sentry
enforces network rules, the Pentacle establishes the namespace, and the
Bottle holds the workload. When you charge a Crucible, all three
containers start in sequence — the Sentry must confirm its iptables
rules before the Bottle can begin.

The project uses a vocabulary-first approach: every concept has a
specific name, and the names carry meaning. A Vessel is a container
specification. A Hallmark is a specific build. A Nameplate ties the
pieces together. The vocabulary is dense but precise — once you learn
the names, the system becomes navigable.

This is the ccyolo bottle — the simplest path into the system. You are
inside a container that can only reach Anthropic. Everything else is
blocked. The sentry is watching. The containment is real.

# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
#
# Recipe Bottle base compose file — static infrastructure
#
# Invoked via:
#   docker compose --env-file .rbk/rbrr.env --env-file .rbk/{moniker}/rbrn.env \
#     -f .rbk/rbob_compose.yml [-f .rbk/{moniker}/compose.yml] -p {moniker} up -d
#
# Env-file variables serve dual purpose:
#   1. YAML interpolation (${VAR} in this file — image refs, subnet, container names)
#   2. Container environment forwarding (bare names in environment: sections)
#
# Network topology:
#   sentry: default (internet via bridge) + enclave (eth0=default priority:1000, eth1=enclave priority:500)
#   pentacle: enclave only (static IP, routes through sentry)
#   bottle: shares pentacle network namespace (network_mode: service:pentacle)
#
# Health chain: sentry healthy → pentacle starts → pentacle healthy → bottle starts (sessile profile)

services:
  sentry:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-sentry
    privileged: true
    networks:
      default:
        priority: 1000
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP}
        priority: 500
    ports:
      - "${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}:${RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION}"
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP
      - RBRR_DNS_SERVER
      - RBRN_ENTRY_MODE
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION
      - RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS
      - RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS
      - RBJE_PROBE
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjh_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  pentacle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL}:${RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-pentacle
    privileged: true
    networks:
      enclave:
        ipv4_address: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP}
    environment:
      - RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP
    command: ["/opt/rbk/rbjp_pentacle.sh"]
    depends_on:
      sentry:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD-SHELL", "test -f /tmp/rbjph_healthy"]
      interval: 2s
      timeout: 5s
      retries: 15
      start_period: 5s

  bottle:
    image: ${RBRR_GCP_REGION}-docker.pkg.dev/${RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/${RBRR_GAR_REPOSITORY}/${RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL}:${RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK}-image
    container_name: ${RBRN_MONIKER}-bottle
    network_mode: "service:pentacle"
    security_opt:
      - label:disable
    cap_drop:
      - NET_RAW
    depends_on:
      pentacle:
        condition: service_healthy
    profiles:
      - sessile

networks:
  enclave:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: ${RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP}/${RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK}

message.content: Can you do that rbob_bottle work in a BCG compliant way?  I see subshells.

content.text:
You're right. `$(id -u)` is a subshell. Let me fix it — BCG uses `id -u` output captured via a variable assignment pattern that avoids the subshell in the export line.

Let me re-read the BCG guidance on this specific pattern.

content.content:
703-|-----------|--------------------------------|----------------------|------------------------------|---------------------------|-------------------------------------|
704-| Predicate | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_predicate` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | 0=true, 1=false              | No (use buc_log_«source») | Never dies, status only             |
705-| Capture   | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_capture`   | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | stdout once at end or exit 1 | No (use buc_log_«source») | Clean error handling, single return |
706-| Enroll    | `[z]«prefix»_[«scope»_]enroll` | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | `z_«funcname»_«retval»` vars | No (use buc_log_«source») | Mutates rolls (parallel arrays) in kindle only; returns via variables |
707-| Recite    | `[z]«prefix»_«what»_recite`    | `z«prefix»_sentinel` | stdout or exit 1             | No (use buc_log_«source») | Read-only access to rolls; never mutates |
708:| Fixture baste | `z«tb»_«name»_baste`         | —                   | — (side effects only)        | Optional                  | Fixture subshell; kindle, source, configure |
709:| Tcase     | `«tc»_«name»_tcase`           | —                    | — (exit status to suite)     | No                        | Case subshell; assertions, verification |
710-| Yawp      | `[z]«prefix»_«name»_yawp`     | `z«module»_sentinel` | `z_«module»_«group»` var    | No                        | Pure assignment, cannot fail, no output |
711-
712----
713-
714-#### Capture Functions (controlled command substitution)
--
772-z«prefix»_file_valid_predicate "${z_config}" || buc_warn "Config invalid, using defaults"
773-```
774-
775-#### Enroll Functions (kindle-only registry population)
776-
777:**Purpose**: Populate parallel-array registries (rolls) during module initialization. Enroll functions solve the subshell problem: when a function both mutates shared state and returns a value, calling it in `$()` loses the mutations.
778-
779-**The parallel-array registry pattern (rolls)**:
780-- Kindle initializes N empty arrays (rolls) of the same length
781-- Enroll validates inputs and appends atomically to all rolls
782-- Recite functions (see below) provide read-only access after kindle completes
--
881-- Caller decides error handling: `|| buc_die` or conditional
882-- Usable anywhere after kindle completes (not restricted to kindle)
883-
884-#### Yawp Functions (pure-computation group return)
885-
886:**Purpose: Produce a value into a shared group return variable via pure bash assignment. No subshells, no stdout, no stderr.**
887-
888:Named for Whitman's "barbaric yawp" — a loud, unmistakeable declaration into a known place. Yawp functions solve the high-frequency value-production problem: when dozens of calls per function each need a computed string, `$()` subshell overhead is disproportionate to the work being done.
889-
890-```bash
891-# «prefix»_«name»_yawp arg1 [arg2] — what it stamps
892-# → local -r z_name="${z_«module»_«group»}"
893-«prefix»_«name»_yawp() {
--
952-
953-The one-yawp-one-capture rhythm is the invariant. Pre-captured constants (e.g., `RBYC_*` from `zrbyc_kindle`) are safe to interpolate directly because they were captured at kindle time — the discipline applies at the yawp call site, not at the interpolation site.
954-
955-**Use yawp functions for:**
956-- Pure string assembly (concatenation, marker stamping)
957:- Value production where `$()` subshell overhead is unnecessary
958-- High-frequency call sites (dozens of calls per function)
959-- Any computed value that uses only bash builtins and cannot fail
960-
961-**Do NOT use yawp functions for:**
962-- Functions that can fail → use `_capture`
--
970-
971-- **_capture**: Need a value that requires command substitution or clean error handling
972-- **_predicate**: Need true/false for conditional logic without dying
973-- **_enroll**: Populate parallel-array registries (rolls) during kindle with validated inputs and return values
974-- **_recite**: Read-only access to roll arrays populated by enroll functions
975:- **_baste**: Fixture preparation inside fixture subshell (kindle, source, configure)
976:- **_tcase**: Test case verification function inside `_tcase` subshell
977-- **_yawp**: Pure-computation value production via group return variable. High-frequency call sites where `$()` overhead is disproportionate. String assembly, marker stamping, path construction.
978-- **Neither**: Use temp files for multi-step pipelines, direct `|| buc_die` for simple failures
979-
980-## Naming Convention Patterns
981-
--
1084-**Note**: Use `=` not `==` in test expressions for POSIX compliance.
1085-
1086-### ❌ Subshell Exit vs Brace-Group
1087-
1088-```bash
1089:# ❌ exit in subshell kills only the subshell — script continues
1090-some_cmd || (echo "ERROR" >&2 && exit 1)
1091-
1092-# ✅ Brace-group stays in same process — exit/return works correctly
1093-some_cmd || { echo "ERROR" >&2; exit 1; }
1094-```
1095-
1096:**Rule**: Error blocks must use `{ ...; }` not `( ... )` when intending to exit or return. The `(subshell)` creates a new process — `exit` terminates only that subshell, not the calling script.
1097-
1098-### ✅ Legitimate Subshell Uses
1099-
1100-The prohibition above is specific to error-handling blocks where `exit`/`return` must reach the calling shell. Subshells are legitimate in these contexts:
1101-
1102-- **Test execution** — principled isolation between suites and cases; see **Test Execution Patterns**
1103-- **`$()` command substitution** — inside `_capture`/`_recite` functions only, or bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`); see **Capture Functions** and **Command Substitution Rules** checklist
1104:- **Isolation subshells** — environment containment with exit-status propagation (below)
1105-
1106-#### Isolation Subshells (environment containment)
1107-
1108:When a sequence of commands mutates the shell environment (sourcing files, `cd`, venv activation, `export`), a subshell prevents those mutations from leaking into subsequent commands. The exit status propagates across the `)` boundary, so the caller can detect and handle failures.
1109-
1110:**Rarely needed.** Most code operates within a single kindled environment. Use isolation subshells only when you must execute commands that change the environment and those changes must not persist.
1111-
1112-**Two variants** — same principle, different caller handling:
1113-
1114-```bash
1115-# Variant 1: Error propagation — die on any internal failure
1116-(
1117-  source "${z_config_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source: ${z_config_file}"
1118-  «prefix»_operation || buc_die "Operation failed"
1119:) || buc_die "Isolation subshell failed"
1120-
1121-# Variant 2: Status capture — caller inspects and decides
1122-local z_status=0
1123-(
1124-  source "${z_config_file}" || buc_die "Failed to source: ${z_config_file}"
1125-  «prefix»_operation || buc_die "Operation failed"
1126-) || z_status=$?
1127-```
1128-
1129:Inside the subshell, `buc_die` calls `exit` which terminates only the subshell — producing a non-zero exit status that the outer `|| buc_die` or `|| z_status=$?` catches. Failure propagates through exit status, not through `exit` reaching the parent.
1130-
1131-**Constraints:**
1132-- Every command inside must have explicit `|| buc_die` — `set -e` is suppressed inside `( ... ) ||` (as with all BCG code, never rely on it)
1133:- The outer boundary must have `|| buc_die` or `|| z_status=$?` — never leave the subshell exit status unchecked
1134:- Sourcing inside the subshell is permitted (this is a primary use case — isolation prevents sourced variables from leaking)

message.content: Wrong, use a temp file to eliminate $(id) subshell
are you sure that is BCG preferred over using temp files?

content.text:
Good catch. Re-reading BCG's "Legitimate Subshell Uses":

> `$()` command substitution — inside `_capture`/`_recite` functions only, or bash introspection (`compgen`, `declare -F`, `mktemp`)

`id -u` is neither a capture function nor bash introspection. Strict BCG reading says temp file:

```bash
local z_uid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_uid"
local z_gid_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbob_host_gid"
id -u > "${z_uid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host UID"
id -g > "${z_gid_file}" || buc_die "Failed to determine host GID"
local z_host_uid=""
z_host_uid=$(<"${z_uid_file}") || buc_die "Failed to read host UID"
local z_host_gid=""
z_host_gid=$(<"${z_gid_file}") || buc_die "Failed to read host GID"
export RBOB_HOST_UID="${z_host_uid}"
export RBOB_HOST_GID="${z_host_gid}"
```

Want me to go with the temp file pattern, or is there a lighter weight approach you'd prefer here?

input.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Align container user UID/GID with host when bind-mounting workspace.
# RBOB_HOST_UID/GID arrive via compose environment from rbob_bottle.sh.
# Vessels without bind mounts simply don't set these — the block is skipped.
if [ -n "${RBOB_HOST_UID:-}" ] && [ -n "${RBRV_USER:-}" ]; then
  usermod  -o -u "${RBOB_HOST_UID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
  groupmod -o -g "${RBOB_HOST_GID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
fi

CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)
chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

# Start SSH daemon in foreground (container lifecycle tied to sshd)
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D

file.content:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure claude user home is ready
CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)

# Seed persistent workspace with sample project on first boot.
# The volume is empty on initial charge; subsequent charges reuse it.
WORKSPACE="${CLAUDE_HOME}/workspace"
if [ -d /opt/ccyolo-sample ] && [ -d "${WORKSPACE}" ] && [ -z "$(ls -A "${WORKSPACE}" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  cp -a /opt/ccyolo-sample/. "${WORKSPACE}/"
  chmod +x "${WORKSPACE}"/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true
fi

chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

# Start SSH daemon in foreground (container lifecycle tied to sshd)
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D

set -e

# Align container user UID/GID with host when bind-mounting workspace.
# RBOB_HOST_UID/GID arrive via compose environment from rbob_bottle.sh.
# Vessels without bind mounts simply don't set these — the block is skipped.
if [ -n "${RBOB_HOST_UID:-}" ] && [ -n "${RBRV_USER:-}" ]; then
  usermod  -o -u "${RBOB_HOST_UID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
  groupmod -o -g "${RBOB_HOST_GID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
fi

CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)
chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Align container user UID/GID with host when bind-mounting workspace.
# RBOB_HOST_UID/GID arrive via compose environment from rbob_bottle.sh.
# Vessels without bind mounts simply don't set these — the block is skipped.
if [ -n "${RBOB_HOST_UID:-}" ] && [ -n "${RBRV_USER:-}" ]; then
  usermod  -o -u "${RBOB_HOST_UID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
  groupmod -o -g "${RBOB_HOST_GID}" "${RBRV_USER}"
fi

CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)
chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

# Start SSH daemon in foreground (container lifecycle tied to sshd)
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# Ensure claude user home is ready
CLAUDE_HOME=$(getent passwd claude | cut -d: -f6)

# Seed persistent workspace with sample project on first boot.
# The volume is empty on initial charge; subsequent charges reuse it.
WORKSPACE="${CLAUDE_HOME}/workspace"
if [ -d /opt/ccyolo-sample ] && [ -d "${WORKSPACE}" ] && [ -z "$(ls -A "${WORKSPACE}" 2>/dev/null)" ]; then
  cp -a /opt/ccyolo-sample/. "${WORKSPACE}/"
  chmod +x "${WORKSPACE}"/*.sh 2>/dev/null || true
fi

chown -R claude:claude "${CLAUDE_HOME}"

# Start SSH daemon in foreground (container lifecycle tied to sshd)
exec /usr/sbin/sshd -D

file.content:
# ccyolo — Claude Code sandbox bottle for Recipe Bottle crucible
#
# SSH-accessible container running Claude Code. The sentry DNAT
# forwards ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION to this container's port 22.
# User SSHs in as "claude" (empty password, PAM-permissive),
# then runs `claude` to authenticate via OAuth copy/paste flow.
#
# Build context: rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ccyolo/ (co-located with Dockerfile)

ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

# Avoid prompts from apt
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Install SSH server and Claude Code operational dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    openssh-server    `# SSH daemon for workstation entry via sentry DNAT`  \
    ca-certificates   `# TLS root certs — required for HTTPS to Anthropic API` \
    curl              `# HTTP client (Claude Code operational dependency)`  \
    git               `# Version control (Claude Code operational dependency)` \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install Claude Code globally via npm (node and npm provided by base image)
# Pin version — v2.1.105+ has a bracketed paste regression that breaks
# OAuth code entry through SSH/docker-exec terminals.
RUN npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code@2.1.89

# Create unprivileged claude user with empty password
RUN useradd -m -s /bin/bash claude && \
    passwd -d claude

# Generate SSH host keys
RUN ssh-keygen -A

# Configure SSH daemon
RUN mkdir -p /var/run/sshd                                       && \
    echo "Port 22"                    >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitRootLogin no"         >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PermitEmptyPasswords yes"   >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config    && \
    echo "PasswordAuthentication yes" >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config

# Configure PAM for permissive authentication (no password prompt)
RUN sed -i 's/@include common-auth/#@include common-auth/' /etc/pam.d/sshd && \
    echo "auth sufficient pam_permit.so" >> /etc/pam.d/sshd

# Ensure PATH includes npm global bin for SSH sessions
RUN echo 'export PATH="/usr/local/bin:$PATH"' >> /etc/bash.bashrc

# Preconfigure Claude Code for full autonomy (yolo mode).
# Inside a network-contained crucible, bypassing permission prompts is
# the correct posture — the sentry enforces the real security boundary.
RUN mkdir -p /home/claude/.claude && \
    echo '{"permissions":{"defaultMode":"bypassPermissions"}}' \
      > /home/claude/.claude/settings.json && \
    chown -R claude:claude /home/claude/.claude

# Stage sample project as a template — the entrypoint seeds the
# persistent workspace volume on first boot.
COPY sample-project/ /opt/ccyolo-sample/

COPY entrypoint.sh /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh
RUN chmod +x /usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh

EXPOSE 22

USER root
ENTRYPOINT ["/usr/local/bin/entrypoint.sh"]

content.text:
Steps 1–6 complete. Now to your question:

**Is `|| buc_die` necessary on `$(<"${z_gid_file}")`?**

`$(<file)` is a bash builtin file-read — it doesn't spawn a subshell. If the file doesn't exist or is unreadable, bash sets `$?` non-zero. But in this context, `id -g` just succeeded writing to that file two lines above — the file is guaranteed present. The `|| buc_die` is defensive but not load-bearing.

That said, the BCG checklist says "NO unguarded `$()` — every `$(cmd)` must have `|| buc_die`". `$(<file)` is technically `$()` syntax even though it's a builtin redirect. So I kept it for BCG compliance. But if you consider `$(<file)` exempt from that rule (it's not a command substitution, it's a bash file-read special form), I can drop the guards.

Your call — keep or remove?

message.content: This sounds like a nit in BCG.  slate a pace in AU to more clearly distinguish $() containing subshells versus builtin redirects; I trust builtin redirects expressly.  Any more work needed on this pace before we test?

input.content:
# jjezs_slate bcg-builtin-redirect-vs-subshell

## Character

Quick editorial clarification — mechanical, not architectural.

## Docket

BCG Command Substitution Rules currently say: "NO unguarded `$()` — every `$(cmd)` must have `|| buc_die`". This conflates two syntactically similar but semantically distinct forms:

- **`$(command)`** — spawns a subshell, runs a command, captures stdout. Can fail. Guard required.
- **`$(<file)`** — bash builtin file redirect. No subshell, no command. Reads file content directly.

Clarify in BCG that `$(<file)` is a builtin redirect exempt from the `|| buc_die` guard requirement. It is not a command substitution despite sharing the `$()` syntax. Add a note in the Command Substitution Rules section distinguishing the two forms.

#
# Recipe Bottle Nameplate - ccyolo
# Claude Code sandbox for network-contained AI development

# Core Service Definition
RBRN_MONIKER=ccyolo
RBRN_DESCRIPTION="Claude Code sandbox with SSH entry, Anthropic-only network access"
RBRN_RUNTIME=docker

# Vessel Source Configuration (GAR vessels)
RBRN_SENTRY_VESSEL=rbev-sentry-deb-tether
RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=rbev-bottle-ccyolo
RBRN_SENTRY_HALLMARK=k260416064318-cbcaa019
RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=k260417070129-f973810d

# Entry Service Configuration (SSH from workstation into bottle)
RBRN_ENTRY_MODE=enabled
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_WORKSTATION=2222
RBRN_ENTRY_PORT_ENCLAVE=22

# Network Uplink Configuration
RBRN_UPLINK_PORT_MIN=10000
RBRN_UPLINK_DNS_MODE=allowlist
RBRN_UPLINK_ACCESS_MODE=allowlist
# example.com is a test target for verifying network containment — proves
# allowlist works for a non-Anthropic domain while everything else stays
# blocked. Currently behind Cloudflare (104.16.0.0/12, 172.64.0.0/13).
# Remove once containment is validated.
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_CIDRS="160.79.104.0/23 104.16.0.0/12 172.64.0.0/13"
RBRN_UPLINK_ALLOWED_DOMAINS="api.anthropic.com claude.ai platform.claude.com example.com"

# Network Configuration
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BASE_IP=10.242.3.0
RBRN_ENCLAVE_NETMASK=24
RBRN_ENCLAVE_SENTRY_IP=10.242.3.2
RBRN_ENCLAVE_BOTTLE_IP=10.242.3.3

# eof

Docket:
  ## Character
  
  Pure mechanical rename for symmetry — wide but shallow. No new features, no design judgment. Establishes a clean tether-only baseline for subsequent airgap additions in `₢A6AAV`. The whole pace is grep-then-sed plus one `git mv`; verification is "does the existing tadmor security suite still pass identically."
  
  ## Docket
  
  Rename `rbev-bottle-ifrit` → `rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether` and migrate its build artifacts into a shared `common-ifrit-context/` directory. Mirrors the existing `common-sentry-context/` pattern. Decision recorded in `₢A6AAI`.
  
  ### Steps
  
  1. Create `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/` directory.
  2. Move source artifacts into the shared context:
     - `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit/src/` → `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/src/`
     - `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit/Cargo.toml` → `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Cargo.toml`
     - `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit/Cargo.lock` → `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Cargo.lock`
  3. Write `rbev-vessels/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether` — current ifrit Dockerfile content. Build logic byte-identical aside from preamble comments updated to reference the shared-context layout.
  4. Rename directory `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit/` → `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether/` (use `git mv` to preserve history).
  5. Rewrite `rbev-vessels/rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether/rbrv.env`:
     - `RBRV_SIGIL=rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether`
     - `RBRV_DESCRIPTION` updated to mention "(tethered)"
     - `RBRV_CONJURE_DOCKERFILE=${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context/Dockerfile.tether`
     - `RBRV_CONJURE_BLDCONTEXT=${RBRR_VESSEL_DIR}/common-ifrit-context`
     - All other fields preserved (mode, egress, reliquary, image origin/anchor, platforms)
  6. Update `.rbk/tadmor/rbrn.env`: `RBRN_BOTTLE_VESSEL=rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether`. Hallmark fields preserved (existing tether hallmark remains valid since the underlying image is unchanged — only the vessel name moves).
  7. Grep sweep for whole-word `rbev-bottle-ifrit` across `Tools/`, `*.adoc`, `*.md`, `.rbk/`, `tt/`, `rbev-vessels/`. Update each occurrence to `rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether`. Anticipated touch sites:
     - Rust source: `Tools/rbk/rbtd/src/rbtdrc_crucible.rs`, possibly `rbtdri_*` infrastructure files
     - Specs: `Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSFR-FundusRegistry.md`, possibly `RBSCC-crucible_charge.adoc`, `RBSIP-ifrit_pentester.adoc`
     - Tabtargets and zipper files (verify any vessel-name parameterized lookups)
     - Paddock A6 vessel table row — defer; will be rewritten at AAI wrap
  8. Verify (in this order):
     - `tt/rbw-cKB.KludgeBottle.tadmor.sh` succeeds — local kludge path unbroken
     - `tt/rbw-cC.Charge.tadmor.sh` charges cleanly with renamed vessel
     - `tt/rbtd-r.Run.tadmor.sh` passes the security suite — case count and outcomes identical to pre-rename baseline
     - `tt/rbtd-s.TestSuite.fast.sh` green
     - `grep -rn "rbev-bottle-ifrit\b" --include="*.sh" --include="*.env" --include="*.rs" --include="*.md" --include="*.adoc"` returns zero hits (no orphaned old name)
  
  ### Requires
  
  - `₢A6AAI` wrapped (decision recorded — provides the architectural rationale for the rename)
  
  ### Out of scope
  
  - Forge vessel creation (`₢A6AAV` Phase 1)
  - Airgap-ifrit vessel creation (`₢A6AAV` Phase 1)
  - `Dockerfile.airgap` creation (`₢A6AAV` Phase 1)
